question: Who had to retrieve water from the Styx, fetch the beauty of Proserpina, and sort a room full of grain into piles, desperate to prove her love for her husband Cupid?

bonuses.q: Psyche was unable to organize the cereals by herself and instead relied on the help of what animals?
What object instructed Psyche on how to fetch Proserpina's beauty from the Underworld?

question: Courted by both Poseidon and Apollo, what deity rejected their advances and swore an oath of chastity that earned her the right to be worshiped in every home and temple's hearth?

bonuses.q: Why is Hestia known as both the first- and last-born of Cronus and Rhea?
bonuses.a: She was the first to be born but last to be disgorged

bonuses.q: What daughter of Daedalion was pursued by both Apollo and Hermes?

question: What god fell hopelessly in love with Orchamus's daughter Leucothoe and spurned Clytie, who transformed into a heliotrope pining after him?

bonuses.q: Leucothoe was buried alive by her father, but Sol transformed her into what type of tree after sprinkling her grave with nectar?
The story of Sol and Leucothoe was told by what king's daughters, who scorned the orgiastic rites of Bacchus in favor of their weaving?

question: What object indirectly caused a son of Portheus's death, was built by Epeius, and was deceptively described as an offering to Minerva by Sinon, persuading Priam to welcome it?

bonuses.q: What name is shared between this son of Portheus and the most famous of the Spartoi, who fathered Pentheus by Agave?
During Sinon's story, he claims that he was persecuted by Ulysses in alliance with what son of Thestor, who would later die of envy at a rival's superior skill?

question: What mythological pair, who met at Marathon when one tried to steal the other's cattle, together carried off the queen Antiope and were trapped in the underworld by the Chairs of Forgetfulness?

bonuses.q: Who was the wife of Pirithous that was abducted by the centaur Eurytion at their wedding?
When Theseus and Pirithous kidnapped Helen, to what city in Attica did they take her?

question: Who wondered why his head-sheep was lagging behind the rest of the flock, not realizing that the sheep was hiding his antagonist Outis, or 'No-one,' who had blinded him?

bonuses.q: To weaken Polyphemus, Odysseus used the wine he got from what priest of Apollo at Ismarus?
What son of Eurymus had warned Polyphemus that someone named Odysseus would wound him?

question: What work, in which Photis bungles a potion, features the inset tale of Cupid and Psyche as heard by its protagonist Lucius while in the form of a donkey?
answer: APULEIUS'S METAMORPHOSES / THE GOLDEN ASS / ASINUS AUREUS

bonuses.q: The Metamorphoses has a surprise ending where Lucius is initiated into the cult of what deity?
Apuleius was persuaded to marry Pudentilla by what friend of his, who was her son?

question: What deity, mocked for the obscurity of her father Coeus by a sister of Pelops, was most offended by the idea that seven sons and seven daughters were better than her twins Apollo and Artemis?

bonuses.q: In Ovid's account, Niobe interrupted a sacrifice to Latona that was set up by what daughter of Tiresias, said to rival her father in the gift of prophecy?
Coeus also fathered what other deity, who was pursued by Zeus and turned into a quail?

question: What deity was supposed to be the heir to Zeus until he was distracted with toys and carried away, after which Athena could only rescue his heart from the Titans who had torn him into pieces?

bonuses.q: Who was abandoned by his mother because she feared her father Crotopus, and was eventually shredded to pieces by dogs?
In order to overtake Arcadia, Pelops pretended friendship with what king, whom he had dismembered and scattered his limbs across the land?

question: Who gave the Balyra river its name after he threw an instrument into it, since after he lost a contest at Dorium in an attempt to lie with the Muses, he was stripped of his musical talent and sight?

bonuses.q: Thamyris was born in what land, home to king Pyreneus who tried to rape the Muses?
Who was the father of Thamyris, a man who died while leading an Argive force against Phlegyan attackers of Delphi?

question: What hero, son of Peleus and the sea-nymph Thetis, was invulnerable everywhere except his heel, where he was finally slain by an arrow guided by Apollo?

bonuses.q: Who shot the arrow that killed Achilles?
What was the name of Achilles' immortal horse, son of the West Wind?

question: What hero traveled to the Underworld to retrieve his wife Eurydice, charming its inhabitants with his music but ultimately losing her when he looked back?

bonuses.q: What instrument did Orpheus play?
What group of women ultimately tore Orpheus apart?

question: Who was the king of the gods in Roman mythology, whose Greek equivalent is Zeus?

bonuses.q: Who was Jupiter's wife and queen of the gods?
What was the name of Jupiter's weapon?

question: What goddess of wisdom and crafts, born fully armored from Jupiter's head, was the Roman equivalent of Athena?

bonuses.q: What bird was sacred to Minerva / Athena?
What city was named after Athena after she won a contest against Poseidon?

question: Who occupied Cappadocia and Bithynia, executed Aquilius with molten gold, and ordered a massacre of 80,000 Romans in Asia in 88 B.C., leading to three wars against him and his kingdom of Pontus?

bonuses.q: What general did Mithridates appoint to capture Greece on his behalf?
What city did Archelaus capture with the help of a revolutionary named Aristion?

question: What man, a protégé of Claudius Pompeianus and former schoolteacher, was asked to take the throne by Aemilius Laetus and Eclectus after his predecessor Commodus was murdered?

bonuses.q: Commodus was killed on the evening of December 31 in what year?
Claudius Pompeianus had previously chosen to stay out of a plot against Commodus by Ummidius Quadratus, Appius Claudius Quintianus, and what woman?

question: Who covered the years 78 to 67 B.C. in a work that showed the fullest development of the inconcinnitās and Thucydidean style of his monographs, Bellum Iugurthīnum and Bellum Catilīnae?

bonuses.q: What work of Tacitus takes remarkably direct inspiration from a battle in the Bellum Iugurthīnum while describing the Battle of Mons Graupius?
Which work of Tacitus is noteworthy for having a far more smooth and flowing style than any of his other works?

question: Give the Latin term for the system, made official in 180 B.C. by the Lex Villia Annalis, which was circumvented by ambitious generals like Pompey who ignored the established prerequisites for holding offices.

bonuses.q: What man, who had started his career well before the minimum age requirements, was found mysteriously dead in 129 B.C., perhaps due to foul play from his wife Sempronia?
Pompey was able to flout age and rank requirements due to the personal loyalty of his army, many of whom came from what specific region in Northeast Italy?

question: Disgruntled at his treatment in Antioch, what late emperor unwisely hastened across the Tigris River to face Shapur II, only to be killed near Ctesiphon, possibly by a Christian in his own ranks?

bonuses.q: To what philosophical school, promoted by Plotinus and Porphyry and common among pagans in the late Empire, did Julian adhere?
Julian's predecessor Constantius II had won a major battle at Mursa against what usurper, who had overthrown his brother Constans?

question: Who executed his predecessor's architect Apollodorus of Damascus and generals Palma and Quietus, set up a cult for a drowned Bithynian youth, and connected the Solway Firth to the Tyne with a wall?

bonuses.q: Hadrian was said to have become emperor thanks to the intervention of what woman, Trajan's wife?
Apollodorus of Damascus angered Hadrian by criticizing what large temple, whose construction the emperor supervised between the Colosseum and the Temple of Peace?

question: Who defeated Egyptians at Panium, alarming Pergamum, and allied with the Aetolians against Rome, though defeats at Myonessus and Magnesia severely harmed his Seleucid Empire?

bonuses.q: What consul, under whom Cato served as military tribune, defeated Antiochus at Thermopylae?
What naval commander from an island off the coast of Anatolia commanded Antiochus's main fleet during the subsequent war?

question: What Roman general crossed the Rubicon river in 49 B.C. with his army, triggering a civil war against Pompey and the Senate?

bonuses.q: What was the name of Caesar's famous account of his wars in Gaul?
On what date was Caesar assassinated?

question: What city is traditionally said to have been founded by Romulus in 753 B.C. and became the capital of a vast empire?

bonuses.q: According to legend, who was Romulus's twin brother?
What she-wolf nursed the infant twins Romulus and Remus?

question: What Roman emperor, the first to hold the title Augustus, ruled from 27 B.C. until his death in 14 A.D., ushering in a period of relative peace known by a Latin name?

bonuses.q: What is the Latin name for the period of peace Augustus inaugurated?
Who was Augustus's great general and son-in-law, builder of the Pantheon?

question: What author was an informer for Nero and the last man he appointed consul, but later began worshiping busts of Vergil and slavishly imitating him in a 17-book epic, Pūnica?

bonuses.q: What Flavian poet composed an epic poem on frāternae aciēs with the aim of following its model, the Aeneid, 'at a distance'?
What poet hailing from Setia was a decemvir who adapted the work of a Hellenistic epic poet?

question: What author depicted the complaint of a walnut tree in Nux, invoked a coprophilic bird in the Ībis, and wrote Epistulae ex Pontō and Trīstia to lament his exile at Tomi?

bonuses.q: What work of 'civil poetry' by Ovid is a poetical calendar meant to illustrate the myths and customs of Latium?
The Fāstī may have drawn on the research of what grammarian from Praeneste?

question: Possibly from Pedum or Gabii, what 'terse and elegant' author describes his love for Marathus and Nemesis in his elegies, which are often contrasted with those of Propertius?

bonuses.q: Tibullus's most famous lover is Delia. What was her real name?
Tibullus's corpus also contains six poems addressed to Neaera by what man?

question: Reproached for a style of 'sand without lime' by Caligula, who nearly condemned him to death, what author described the daily practice of Stoicism to his friend Lucilius in 124 Epistulae Mōrālēs?

bonuses.q: Seneca wrote nine surviving works of what type of fābula, which are the only extant representatives of the genre since Pacuvius and Accius are lost?
Which of Seneca's tragedies, sharing its title with Ennius's final work, is the only one without an extant Greek model?

question: What poet popularized a philosopher from Messina, criticized the faunī vātēsque who had written in Saturnians, and used his Hedyphagetica and Annālēs to introduce dactylic hexameter to Latin?

bonuses.q: Ennius versified the Aetolian campaign of Fulvius Nobilior with what fābula praetexta, his only known one aside from Sabinae?
What controversial claim did the philosopher Euhemerus make about the gods?
bonuses.a: THAT THEY WERE / HAD BEEN MORTALS

question: What work rejects the 'words that persecuted Lycambes' of the Parian poet that inspired it, but wishes a shipwreck on a bad poet, depicts the witch Canidia, and playfully mocks its author's patron Maecenas?

bonuses.q: Who is the poetaster who receives an inverted propempticon in the Epodes?
About what man does Horace write that his whole life 'stands before one's eyes, as if it were painted on a votive tablet'?

question: What Roman poet wrote the Aeneid, an epic poem about the Trojan hero Aeneas and the founding of Rome, left unfinished at his death in 19 B.C.?

bonuses.q: What are the names of Vergil's two other major works?
In the Aeneid, who is the Carthaginian queen who falls in love with Aeneas?

question: What Roman orator and statesman, famous for his speeches against Catiline beginning 'Quo usque tandem,' also wrote philosophical works and the defense speech Pro Archia?

bonuses.q: What four speeches did Cicero deliver against Catiline?
bonuses.a: THE CATILINARIAN ORATIONS / IN CATILINAM I-IV

bonuses.q: In what year was Cicero executed on the orders of Mark Antony?

question: Excluding prefixes, the English word 'kerchief' derives from what two Latin words, one of which means 'to cover' and the other of which is at the root of 'chief,' 'precipitate,' and 'capitulate'?

bonuses.q: From what two Latin nouns do we derive 'jeopardy'?
From what two Latin words do we derive 'hidalgo'?

question: Biological experiments are described as conducted either in vīvō or with what other Latin phrase, which denotes that they take place under artificial conditions and literally means 'in glass'?

bonuses.q: Give the Latin and English for the medical abbreviation q.s.
bonuses.a: QUANTUM SATIS / QUANTUM SUFFICIT — AS MUCH AS IS ENOUGH

bonuses.q: What is the Latin for the abbreviation sc., and what does it mean in context?
bonuses.a: SCILICET — understand that the following word is implied

question: What Latin noun, a cognate of the first part of Iuppiter, is regularly feminine when used of a specific or fixed time, but is otherwise one of the only fifth-declension nouns to be masculine?

bonuses.q: What grammatical term refers to nouns that vary in gender, such as diēs and epulum?
What masculine noun regularly becomes neuter in the plural except when it means 'topics of argument' or 'passages from books'?

question: What two Latin adjectives are technically neither singular nor plural, but dual, as evidenced by endings like -ōbus and their meanings of 'both' and 'two,' respectively?

bonuses.q: If I say that I have ter dēnī of something, how many do I have?
Using the same formula for multiplication, say 'four times two.'

question: Phrases meaning 'from that point,' 'at that time,' 'what news,' and 'two thousand soldiers' — inde locī, id temporis, quid novī, and duo mīlia mīlitum — all exhibit what use of the genitive?

bonuses.q: What use of the genitive is often seen in words like capitis, maiestātis, and repetundārum?
What use of the genitive can be replaced by a prepositional phrase with ergā?

question: Of the verbs suādeō, dēficiō, ignoscō, īrascor, and faveō, which does NOT take the dative, even though its meaning of 'fail' might suggest that?

bonuses.q: Say in good Latin using parcō: 'They are not spared.' You will need an impersonal passive.
Say in good Latin using mīnor: 'I threaten them with death.'

question: What Latin phrase, used in legal and academic contexts, means 'other things being equal'?

bonuses.q: What Latin phrase means 'in the year of the Lord' and is used in dating?
What does the Latin abbreviation 'i.e.' stand for, and what does it mean?

question: What Latin phrase meaning 'from the founding of the city' is abbreviated A.U.C. and used to date events from Rome's founding?
answer: AB URBE CONDITA / ANNO URBIS CONDITAE

bonuses.q: What does the Latin phrase 'veni, vidi, vici' mean in English?
bonuses.a: I CAME, I SAW, I CONQUERED

bonuses.q: What Latin word do we use in English to mean 'and the rest' or 'and so on'?

question: What three-word Vergilian phrase meaning 'a woman was the leader of the deed' emphasizes Dido's remarkable ability to organize her flight from Tyre in Book 1 of the Aeneid?

bonuses.q: In Book 6, what three-word phrase does the Sibyl use to warn Aeneas that entering the underworld is much easier than escaping? It idiomatically means 'once you do this, there's no turning back.'
What far-north island fills in the blank of the Vergilian phrase ultima [blank], used today to describe any exciting frontier?

question: What Latin phrase plays off of dating systems to express that something is not possible and emphatically means 'never'?

bonuses.q: What Latin phrase, literally meaning 'you are weaving a little rope out of sand,' is used to indicate the pursuit of something impossible?
What Latin phrase literally meaning 'wool from a donkey' similarly denotes an impossible task?

question: What Latin noun is the O in Frederick III's motto A.E.I.O.U. — Austriae est imperāre [blank] universō — and appears in papal blessings addressed 'urbī et [blank],' meaning 'to the city and to the world'?

bonuses.q: Catholic depictions of the crucifixion often bear a titulus reading INRI. What is the full Latin for this abbreviation?
Give the Latin abbreviation used in place of the English 'B.C.' for expressing a year relative to Jesus's birth.

question: Translate the following quote by Augustine into English: 'Melius est reprehendant nōs grammaticī, quam nōn intellegant populī.'
answer: IT IS BETTER THAT THE GRAMMARIANS CRITICIZE US THAN THAT THE PEOPLE DON'T UNDERSTAND US

bonuses.q: Rewrite that sentence to use infinitives rather than subjunctive verbs.
bonuses.a: MELIUS EST REPREHENDERE NOS GRAMMATICOS QUAM NON INTELLEGERE POPULOS

bonuses.q: Translate this punning sentence by Augustine: 'In nostrā disertitūdine vōs dēsertī eritis.'
bonuses.a: IN OUR ELOQUENCE YOU WILL BE DESERTED / ABANDONED

question: What Latin noun follows either audentīs or fortīs in famous quotes by Vergil and Terence respectively, completing the sentiment that fortune favors such people?

bonuses.q: Give the idiomatic meaning of the Juvenalian quote: frontis nulla fidēs.
Give the idiomatic meaning of this Horace quote about travel: caelum nōn animum mūtant quī trans mare currunt.
bonuses.a: TRAVELING CHANGES YOUR LOCATION, NOT YOUR MINDSET

question: What famous Latin phrase, attributed to Julius Caesar after his swift victory at Zela in 47 B.C., means 'I came, I saw, I conquered'?

bonuses.q: What does the Latin motto 'e pluribus unum' mean, as found on U.S. currency?
What does 'carpe diem' mean, and from which Roman poet does it come?

question: What Latin phrase meaning 'in good faith' is commonly used in English to describe sincere, honest intentions?

bonuses.q: What Latin phrase meaning 'by the very fact' describes something that is true by definition or as a direct consequence?
What Latin phrase meaning 'after the fact' describes something done or occurring after an event?

question: What Latin phrase, used on the U.S. Great Seal, means 'He has favored our undertakings' and refers to divine blessing on America?

bonuses.q: What other Latin phrase appears on the U.S. Great Seal's reverse, meaning 'a new order of the ages'?
What Latin phrase meaning 'out of many, one' also appears on the Great Seal?
